title: Video generation models as world simulators
hook: Imagine an AI that dreams up videos by simulating how the world unfolds.
promise: You will understand how modern video models act as world simulators by generating spacetime patches.
prerequisite_note: Bring a curiosity about how computers can imagine future frames and events in moving images.

glossary.plain: A chunk of video that covers a small region in space and time.
A neural network that tries to predict or simulate the dynamics of the real world.
Patterns that show how model performance improves as it gets bigger or trains on more data.
The ability of a model to keep events logical and objects coherent across many video frames.
A way of generating data step by step, using previous outputs as context for new ones.
How well generated videos follow the rules of real-world physics.

cards.title: Can AI imagine video worlds?
body: See how new models generate not just frames, but entire worlds that unfold over time.

cards.title: How does an AI dream up a video?
body: Imagine describing a scene and watching an AI invent a video of it, complete with moving objects and unfolding action.
Early video models often created short, blurry clips. Newer models try to understand and simulate the world, creating richer, longer, and more believable videos.

cards.title: From patches to worlds
body: First, the model breaks video into spacetime patches, like tiny moving tiles.
Next, it learns to predict what comes next in each patch, using context from the whole scene.
Finally, by stitching these predictions together, the model simulates how a scene unfolds, creating long, coherent videos.
cards.caption: The model builds videos by simulating small patches, then assembling them into a believable world.

cards.title: Old frame-by-frame vs. world simulation
body: Switch between the old approach and the world simulator to see the difference in video quality and coherence.
cards.offNote: Videos often become jumbled or lose objects over time.
cards.onNote: Videos stay logical, with objects and physics remaining consistent for longer stretches.

cards.title: The leap: scaling video as a world model
body: The big breakthrough is treating video generation as a world simulation problem, not just an image prediction task.
By scaling up models and data, and focusing on predicting spacetime patches, the system learns to simulate how events unfold with realistic consistency.

cards.title: How much world understanding does the model need?
body: Adjust how much the model 'knows' about the world to see the impact on video realism.
cards.low: Objects flicker and physics break down quickly.
cards.mid: Short scenes look good, but oddities appear over time.
cards.high: Videos stay logical and objects behave realistically across many frames.

cards.title: What makes a world-simulating video model?
body: Pick the six essential ingredients for generating believable video worlds.

choices.label: Spacetime Patch Prediction
choices.note: Lets the model focus on small, local changes in video.

choices.label: Large-scale Training Data
choices.note: Exposes the model to diverse events and scenes.

choices.label: Autoregressive Architecture
choices.note: Enables step-by-step prediction, building videos over time.

choices.label: Physical Consistency Loss
choices.note: Encourages the model to obey physics-like rules.

choices.label: Temporal Attention
choices.note: Helps the model link events across many frames.

choices.label: Scaling Model Size
choices.note: Improves the model's ability to capture complex world dynamics.

cards.title: How much better are world simulators?
body: World-simulating models produce longer, more realistic videos with fewer glitches than older methods.
They achieve higher scores on benchmarks for temporal consistency, physical plausibility, and visual quality.

bars.label: Long-term consistency
bars.note: Objects stay coherent for more frames

bars.label: Visual quality
bars.note: Generated frames look sharper and more detailed

cards.caption: World simulators outperform previous models on realism, coherence, and length of generated videos.

cards.title: Why world simulation changed video AI
body: This approach helped video models break free from short, blurry clips by teaching them to simulate how the world behaves.
It set the stage for AI systems that can generate rich, long, and physically coherent videos, moving closer to true imagination.

recap.label: Predict
recap.note: Use context to generate the next patch.

recap.label: Assemble
recap.note: Stitch patches into a coherent video.

recap.label: Simulate
recap.note: Model events so videos follow real-world logic.

cards.title: Video generation models as world simulators
body: Read the full paper for technical details, experiments, and future directions in video world simulation.
